author: doll-agent
content:
Implemented in ferray-linalg/src/gemm/mod.rs as a sign-extending wrapper around the i16 kernel:

```rust
unsafe fn gemm_i8_signed(m, n, k, a: *const i8, b: *const i8, c: *mut i32, accumulate: bool) -> bool
```

**Approach**: i8 inputs are widened to i16 (sign-extension) into temporary buffers, then dispatched to gemm_avx2_i16. This matches the vpmovsxbw + vpmaddwd pattern in OpenBLAS, except we materialize the widened operands once instead of per K-pair.

Trade-off: 2× memory bandwidth on input vs in-kernel widening, but reuses the proven i16 4×8 tile + packing + parallel driver. Future optimization: dedicated i8 kernel that does vpmovsxbw inside the inner loop to avoid the widen pass.

**Tests** (integer_tests.rs): 2 passing — full signed range incl. negatives matches naive ref; mixed pos/neg pattern at non-multiple dims.

Verified: cargo test -p ferray-linalg --release, cargo clippy -D warnings clean.
